title: Radio interview about volunteering
explanation:
Radio interview about volunteering

Listen to a short interview.

Transcript

Host: Why did you start volunteering at the food bank?
Guest: At first, I wanted to gain experience for my CV, but I soon realised how important the work was. We sort donations, prepare food parcels and talk to families who need support.
Host: Has the experience changed you?
Guest: Definitely. I have become more patient and much more aware of problems in my community.

questions.text: The guest volunteers at [*a food bank|a hospital|an art gallery].
questions.feedback: The place is a food bank.

questions.text: At first, the guest wanted [*experience for a CV|free meals|a part-time job].
They sort donations and prepare [*food parcels|radio equipment|school lessons].
The experience made the guest more [*patient|ambitious|suspicious].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The guest also became more aware of problems in the local ____.  | *community | airport | industry
Families who need ____ receive help from the food bank. | *support | publicity | transport
The guest says the work is very ____.  | *important | formal | easy
questions.feedback: They realised how important it was.

questions.text: The host asks whether the experience has ____ the guest. | *changed | hired | judged
The interview shows that volunteering can be [*personally valuable|a waste of time|financially rewarding only].
questions.feedback: The guest describes positive personal change.

questions.text: The food bank helps [*local families|international tourists|shop owners].
The guest's motivation [*developed over time|never changed|was unclear].
questions.feedback: It began as CV experience and became more meaningful.

questions.text: The tone is [*positive and reflective|angry|formal and distant].
